Hey, welcome back to part three of the introduction. Shirt sleeves rolled up, ready to rock and roll with the story of how I invented the straight line. And the reason that's irrelevant is because I was actually using another system before I invented the straight line. It didn't have a name and that system itself was actually really good. You see what happened was is I stumbled upon an untapped niche in the stock market. We're selling $5 stocks to the richest 1% of Americans. If for whatever reason no one had tried it before and when I tested the idea myself, the results was so profound I decided to reinvent the firm. You see we were selling penny stocks to average moms and pops before that and that old system I had was good enough for that. In other words, it's not very difficult to get on the phone with an average mom or dad who wants to put $500 into a crazy flyer stock. It's mostly an impulse buy and you can kind of hype it up and a little bit of bullshit and it works. And my guys were experts at that and the system I have was really good. Tonality, had an overcome objections, all the basic stuff that you read about even today with outside the straight line system. But here's the deal, like with any system, you don't know how great it really is until you stress it to the point of fracture. And that's what happened with this because when I stressed it by trying to have my 12 ninkle poops called the richest 1% of Americans, they couldn't do it. The system broke down. So everything I had taught them that it actually worked up to that point. They were making about 8,000, 10,000 a month, which is pretty good back in the day, right? 1988, 89, right? There's a lot of money back then. But they could not use that same system and close rich people. And it was driving me crazy. I mean, just see, understand, here's what's happening. I'm doing it. I'm testing it myself and having massive success. My junior partner Danny, who I trained, who was a born closer like me, he was having massive success with it. So Danny, I'm killing it. And the 12 ninkle poops are calling the same people, trying to use the same pictures I wrote, sell the same stocks. They couldn't close even one person. And just so you know, I was already known as a top sales trainer back then. But when they failed to be able to close rich people, I went on a quest for like a month. I went out there and started seeking out every sales trainer out there, every system out there. I flew across the country. I read every book, listened to every tape. And here's what I found. My old system was so much better than all the stuff crap that was out there at the time and needed to this day that I had nowhere to go. In other words, my stuff wasn't working. And when I looked for all the other best stuff out there, my stuff blew it away. It was nothing even compared to it. So I was like, what do I do now? How do I get these 12 guys to close rich people? It was driving me crazy. It was driving the guys crazy too, because here's what happens. When you have a sales force that's not closing, they want to quit. They get negative, right? So I would do these marathon sessions every two weeks or so on a Tuesday or Wednesday. They come back to the firm and just do a marathon for three or four hours. And every morning in between, I would do about a one hour training and every afternoon, about a 90 minute training to close out the day. But none of that was working. So now we flash forward here to this magical Tuesday evening and they were like, God, please come back and give us a marathon. We can't close. We're going crazy. And I just, I knew they were about to quit here. So they're like, fine, I'll do it. I'll do it. So I went home, took a shower, right? I went back to the firm at night 7 p.m. and I stood in front of the Stratonites and you should have seen the glum looks on these kids' faces because they hadn't closed it over a month. And meanwhile, I'm like going nuts because Danny and I are making it a fortune. And I'm like, guys, I don't get it. What is so hard about this? I'm doing it. Danny's doing it. You can do it. Do tell me, what's the problem? And at first it was just like sort of dead sounds, fearful expressions because I was so angry. And then finally one of them spit sack goes, there's too many objections. They keep hitting us with objections. And another goes, yeah, there's thousands of objections. And another guy, thousands. I'm like, thousands, really? Like, yeah, there's thousands of objections. We can't even get our pitch off. I'm like, really, thousands of objections. Great. Let's write them all down. Come on. I have my whiteboard behind me. I'm saying, I want to write down every objection. My plan was, was that I was going to write all of them down and then one by one show them how I would overcome these objections and close the deal. That was my plan. So I said, come on, who's first spit one out? After a bit of silence, I had these fearful, I was like going to ring their necks. That was my expression. So finally one guy goes, they want to think about it. I said, great. Think about it. I wrote that on the board. What else? Call back. I said, great. Call back. I wrote that on the board. What else? It's a bad time of year one calls. I said, bad time of year. I wrote that on the board, right? And on and on, there were those four objection guys. You have 996 to go. Let's keep going to get to a thousand objections. And they really went at it. By the way, they just started giving me objection after objection after objection. Every time they gave me one, I wrote it on the board. What's next? What's next? And finally, all right, I had exhausted every conceivable objection out there. The board was filled with objections, which at the end of the day, you ready for this? Turned out to be 14 objections. 14, not thousands. I was like, oh my, I literally, I'm like, I don't think you guys know how angry I'm at. You've been whining about these so-called thousands of objections. Meanwhile, there's only 14 of them. And even those don't matter. Half of them are duplicates of two. They're, let me speak to someone else. I was in my wife, my lawyer, my partner, the butcher, the baker, the cantaloupe, you get it? Or it's a bad time of year. It's Christmas time, back to school time, groundhogs day in Leithia. And so half of them were variations of two. And then some thought hit me and I said, you know, like even those don't matter. And I looked at these guys and said, guys, you guys are unbelievable. They're whining about all these objections. There's only 14. Half of them are repeats. But even those don't matter. I mean, don't you guys get it? And POP, a thought comes in my head. I said, every sale is the same. And they're like, what? You can see by their expressions, they're like, every sale is not the same. Every sale is different. Like, guys, every sale is the same. Watch. And another visual pops in my head. I said, it's a straight line. I turned to the board and drew this long, thin, straight line across the center of the board and put a big, thick X on either end and said, this is your open, where the sale begins, and this is your close, where the client says, yeah, let's do it. And just like that, what happened was something clicked inside my head. And you know, if you read the book, Think, Giggle, Rich, you talk about this thing called the collective unconscious where you have access to all that there is in the universe. So all of a sudden, the floodgates opened up for me and this system, this idea started coming to me. I wanted to have a different, entirely unique way to train people and to describe a sale in the sense that a sale was basically a straight line. It was an open, there was a close, and the shortest distance between any two points is a straight line. It was a perfect sale, right? And what happened was there was something about this visual of a straight line. And from my perspective, you see, because that was so natural a closing, to me, every sale was the same. And I'll explain why when we get to the core learning modules. But my mind was so simple, every sale is the same. I do the same things every time. And when I said that, and I spread it out on the context of a line, it was like, it was just like the floodgates opened up, it was like, monitor from heaven. And after that, four, five hours worth of stuff was just coming out of my mouth, like, pearls of sales wisdom. And when I left that night, everyone was in awe. I was kind of in awe, but I was wondering, was this really effective? I wonder what was going to happen the next morning, because we weren't just calling, we were just training, right? Well next morning, I came back to work 9am. I'm like, exciting. I hope this really broke through. And at 9.30 when they pick up their phones, they start dialing. And these 12, or should I say, formerly 12 barely post-adolescent ninkle poops, well, they went on an account opening spree of such biblical proportions that within a matter of weeks, they were all million dollar producers, kids were pouring in from all over the country to hop on the straight line grade, be traitors. And ultimately, it turned into a movie, it was leading out of the Caprio, Mardi's courses. I mean, it was so insane. It was all because of that one night that I had this thought about a straight line and the ability to essentially take control of the conversation and make every sale the same. And that is what you're going to learn in these learning modules going forward. So that's the story, the creation of the straight line system, and now it's your turn. And just so you know, remember it's been scrubbed through the highest level of ethics and integrity and also the language pattern themselves. I've worked with them for years now. They're infinitely more effective. This truly can turn you into a world-class closer, a top producer in your field in a very short period of time. So dive in and watch what happens and believe me, your life will never be the same. Welcome aboard.
